prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial begins with a black screen.

Around 11.9 seconds, it cuts to a tightly framed, frontal close-up of a young light-skinned woman against a plain pale grey background. She has straight, medium-length blonde hair, minimal visible styling, and a serious, neutral facial expression. She looks directly into the camera. The image is framed with black borders around it.

Around 37.1 seconds, the visual changes to another centered close-up of what appears to be the same or a very similar woman, now presented with more polished styling. Her hair is fuller, wavy, and voluminous, and her face appears more made-up or brightly lit. She remains facing forward, looking directly at the viewer with a neutral, composed expression. The background is still very light and plain.

At the end, around 73.0 seconds, the screen shows a simple white background with a blue graphic of three flying dove shapes. Beneath or beside the birds is blue text reading: “the dove self-esteem fund.” The final frame functions as a logo or campaign identification screen. No other visual information is present.
